task_completion.description: Open gvSIG Desktop and create a new View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The gvSIG Desktop application is open, and a 'View' window or workspace is visible in the main interface.

task_completion.description: Add the Countries shapefile to the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A layer containing 'countries' or 'admin_0' in its name is visible in the Table of Contents (TOC) on the left, and polygon shapes representing world countries are rendered in the map view.

task_completion.description: Add the Rivers shapefile to the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A layer containing 'rivers' or 'centerlines' in its name is visible in the TOC, and line shapes representing rivers are rendered in the map view.

task_completion.description: Add the Populated Places / Cities shapefile to the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A layer containing 'cities' or 'populated_places' in its name is visible in the TOC, and point shapes representing cities are rendered in the map view.

task_completion.description: Arrange the layers in the correct draw order (Cities on top, Rivers in middle, Countries on bottom).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Table of Contents (TOC), the layers must be listed in this exact order from top to bottom: Cities/populated_places (top), Rivers/centerlines (middle), Countries/admin_0 (bottom). Visually, the points and lines should appear on top of the country polygons.

task_completion.description: Save the project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/projects/reference_map.gvsproj', OR the main gvSIG window title bar displays 'reference_map.gvsproj'.

integrity.description: The agent used the gvSIG Desktop GUI to build the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the gvSIG graphical interface (menus, buttons, dialogs) rather than a terminal window writing XML/gvsproj files directly.

integrity.description: The map view displays actual geographic data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The map view shows distinct geographic features (polygons, lines, points) corresponding to the real-world data, not an imported static image or empty bounding boxes.

integrity.description: The agent did not spoof the save path in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/projects/reference_map.gvsproj' is seen in the actual gvSIG save dialog or title bar, not typed into a standalone text editor or terminal echo command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target project file must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/gvsig_data/projects/reference_map.gvsproj'. The map must include exactly three layers with specific keywords in their names: 'countries' or 'admin_0' (polygons), 'rivers' or 'centerlines' (lines), and 'cities' or 'populated_places' (points). The correct draw order in the gvSIG Table of Contents (from top to bottom) is: Cities, Rivers, Countries.
